conceptualization.theme_description: Outline the bright lights and dark shadows of city life after sunset.

steps.description: Begin by selecting a high-quality, cold-pressed watercolor paper that can handle heavy washes without warping.
Choose a palette that reflects the dynamic contrast of a city at night. Incorporate shades of deep blues, stark blacks, metallic silvers, and bursts of neon-like yellows and reds.
Start by laying down a wet wash of dark blues and blacks to establish the night sky and the base for the darker areas of the buildings. This sets the mood for the painting.

While the first layer is still damp, introduce the brighter colors—the yellows, reds, and silvers to mimic the city lights. The water on the paper will allow the colors to bleed into each other naturally, giving a glowing effect to the lights.
Use a technique called 'lifting' by employing a clean, dry brush to softly remove some of the wet paint. This can create lighter areas that suggest illuminated windows and streetlights.
Add soft, abstract shapes to represent cars and moving traffic by dropping in diluted pigments and gently directing them with a horizontal brush stroke or tilting the paper to let the pigment run.

Let the painting fully dry. Once the initial abstraction is dry, reinforce some of the brighter areas if needed, adding more vivid splashes or making minor corrections to maintain the balance between light and dark.
To finish, softly edge some of the light features with a very thin layer of white or light grey, enhancing the glowing effect against the dark background.
Evaluate the piece from a distance to ensure it accurately captures the lively, yet mysterious character of a cityscape at night.
